description: Bitcoin Core bench_bitcoin performance comparison: upstream libsecp256k1 vs UltrafastSecp256k1 shim
reproduction: git clone https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin && git checkout v28.0 && git apply docs/INTEGRATION_PATCH.patch && cmake -B build -DUSE_ULTRAFAST_SECP256K1=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS='-march=native' && cmake --build build -j$(nproc) && build/src/bench/bench_bitcoin -s 3

bench_config.shim_config: w=18 precomputed table cache, CT signing path (ct::ecdsa_sign_hedged)

results.note: ECDSA transaction signing; UltrafastSecp256k1 hedged nonce via ct::ecdsa_sign_hedged

methodology: bench_bitcoin run with -s 3 (3-second stable run per benchmark). Results averaged over multiple trials. Shim uses CT-safe signing path (ct::ecdsa_sign_hedged for ndata != nullptr) and precomputed w=18 generator table.
